description: Generate and draw randomised orthogonal 'walks' in the style of Marcus John Henry Brown's 90 Waypoint Walk as a vanilla-TypeScript Canvas app. A fresh reinterpretation each generation (random turn sequence, segment lengths, wildcards), rendered as a clean orthogonal map capped to a single A4 page. Built with DDD + hexagonal architecture (pure, headless-testable domain core) per docs/adr/0001-0006.

userStories.title: Scaffold the project (build, test, dev, serve)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a TypeScript project skeleton with build and test tooling so subsequent stories have somewhere to land and a way to be verified.
acceptanceCriteria: package.json has no runtime dependencies; dev deps limited to TypeScript and the node:test runner
tsconfig.json compiles to ES modules with strict enabled; domain/ and application/ do NOT include the 'dom' lib
Directory layout created: src/domain/, src/application/, src/adapters/, src/main.ts, and a tests location
npm scripts work: build (tsc), test (headless node:test), dev (watch), serve (Node static server on port 8000, auto-incrementing to the next free port if taken)
index.html loads the compiled entry via <script type="module">
npm run build and npm test both succeed against a trivial placeholder test
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Scaffold present: project-reference tsconfigs (core has no DOM lib), ESM/NodeNext, strict. index.html loads dist/src/main.js as a module. src/domain/scaffold.ts + tests/scaffold.test.ts are the trivial placeholder (build->headless-test pipeline). serve.mjs auto-increments 8000->8001 (verified). The reset commit (29db285) left US-002..005 test files behind; removed them as they belong to their own stories and import not-yet-rebuilt src modules.

userStories.title: Point and Bounds value objects
userStories.description: As a developer, I need immutable geometry primitives so the domain can reason about coordinates and the generation-space bounding box.
acceptanceCriteria: Point is immutable (x, y) and supports equality and translation
Bounds is a generation-space rectangle supporting contains(point) with a padding inset and grow(factor)
No DOM/Canvas references
Unit tests cover equality, containment with padding, and growth
Tests pass (npm test)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Heading and Turn value objects
userStories.description: As a developer, I need direction primitives so the generator can apply turns and walk in a heading.
acceptanceCriteria: Heading enumerates North/East/South/West; North points up (decreasing y)
Turn enumerates Left (L) and Right (R)
Applying L rotates the heading 90 degrees counter-clockwise; R rotates 90 degrees clockwise
Heading exposes its unit step vector
Unit tests verify all four headings x both turns and the unit vectors
Tests pass (npm test)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Segment value object
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a straight orthogonal segment with the geometry queries the layout rules depend on.
acceptanceCriteria: Segment joins two Points and is always purely horizontal or vertical (construction rejects diagonals)
Exposes length, orientation (horizontal/vertical), distance-from-a-point, and a parallel-overlap query (overlapping range + separation distance)
No DOM/Canvas references
Unit tests cover length, orientation, point-distance, and parallel overlap/separation
Tests pass (npm test)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: TurnSequence value object
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the ordered L/R sequence that defines a walk's shape, randomisable per generation.
acceptanceCriteria: TurnSequence is an immutable ordered list of Turn values
For a walk of N waypoints the sequence has exactly N-2 turns
A factory generates a random sequence of a given length from an injected RandomSource (may be stubbed in tests)
Unit tests verify length math for N=10 and N=90 and deterministic output under a seeded source
Tests pass (npm test)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Waypoint entity
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the waypoint entity carrying its sequence number, position, outbound turn, and wildcard state.
acceptanceCriteria: Waypoint has a sequence number, a Point position, an outbound turn (L/R or none), and a wildcard flag
The first and last waypoints have no outbound turn and are never wildcards
A wildcard waypoint models heading-unchanged (turn-skipped) semantics
Unit tests cover first/last having no turn, the wildcard flag, and wildcards confined to waypoints 2..N-1
Tests pass (npm test)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: layout-rules pure constraint predicates
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the full set of spacing/overlap constraints as pure predicates so both the generator's hot loop and the Walk invariant can share them.
acceptanceCriteria: Pure functions (no exceptions) returning booleans / violation lists for: no two waypoint circles (radius 25px) overlap
No two parallel segments with overlapping range are closer than 55px
No path segment passes closer than 35px from any non-adjacent waypoint centre
No segment passes through a non-adjacent waypoint circle
Every turn label's fixed NE (45 degrees), 46px-from-centre position has at least 8px clearance from non-adjacent segments
All waypoints lie within bounds (30px padding from edges)
No DOM/Canvas references
Unit tests include positive and negative cases for each constraint, including boundary distances (e.g. 55px vs 54px)
Tests pass (npm test)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Walk always-valid aggregate root
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Walk that cannot exist in an invalid state, so no caller has to remember to validate it (docs/adr/0004).
acceptanceCriteria: Walk.create(...) composes the layout-rules predicates into an invariant and throws on any violation; there is no isValid() method
A valid Walk exposes its waypoints, segments, generation-space bounding box, and cumulative distance from the start per waypoint
The aggregate is built from a finished placement, not incrementally (no draft Walk)
Unit tests: a known-good layout constructs; layouts violating each invariant throw
Tests pass (npm test)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: src/domain/walk.ts: Walk aggregate, always-valid by construction. Walk.create(waypoints, bounds) runs structural checks (>=2 waypoints, sequence 1..N in order, totalWaypoints===N), DERIVES segments from consecutive waypoints (Segment ctor rejects diagonals), then composes layout-rules.checkLayout into the invariant and throws on any violation. No isValid(). Exposes waypoints, segments (N-1), boundingBox (tight gen-space box), cumulativeDistances[] (per-waypoint from start), plus waypointCount/totalDistance/cumulativeDistanceTo(i). bounds param is the GENERATION REGION for the within-bounds(30px) check; boundingBox is the separate tight box for rendering. tests/walk.test.ts: 17 tests - known-good 4-wp walk constructs; each of the 6 layout rules + 4 structural rules throw. Fixtures verified to isolate their target rule (rule4 also fires rule3 since the 25px check is subsumed by the 35px one).

userStories.title: Driven ports and a seedable RandomSource
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the two real boundaries (randomness, rendering) plus a cooperative-yield boundary inverted as ports, with a deterministic random source for tests (docs/adr/0003, 0006).
acceptanceCriteria: Port interfaces defined in the domain/application layer: RandomSource (e.g. nextInt, nextFloat), Renderer (draw a Walk with display options), and a Yield port exposing a single yieldToEventLoop() method

A seedable RandomSource implementation produces a deterministic stream from a given seed; the same implementation serves both tests (fixed seed) and production (entropy by default, or a URL-supplied seed per US-022)
No DOM/Canvas types in the port definitions
Unit tests verify the seeded source is reproducible and the range helpers are correct
Tests pass (npm test)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: src/domain/random-source.ts: expanded interface with nextInt(min,max). src/domain/seeded-random.ts: SeededRandom class (mulberry32 PRNG) implements RandomSource — same class for tests (fixed seed) and production (entropy-seeded default). src/application/renderer-port.ts: Renderer port (draw/clear, DisplayOptions) + src/application/yield-port.ts: Yield port (yieldToEventLoop). No DOM/Canvas in any port. tests/seeded-random.test.ts: 9 tests covering reproducibility, [0,1) range, nextInt bounds, coverage of endpoints. tests/turn-sequence.test.ts: stubSource updated to also implement nextInt (TurnSequence only calls nextFloat, stub satisfies the expanded interface). 193 tests total pass.

userStories.title: walk-generator domain service (bounded generation, lookahead, re-roll)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the core generator that places a random turn sequence under the layout constraints and is guaranteed to terminate (docs/adr/0002, 0004, 0006).
acceptanceCriteria: walkGenerator.generate(count, randomSource) is a pure synchronous generator function (function*) that yields a small progress value roughly every batch (~50 attempts, or once per re-roll) and returns either a valid Walk or a failure signal
Works on a lightweight mutable placement buffer; wraps the finished placement into Walk.create(...) at the end (never builds a draft Walk)
Walk shape: starts facing North; segment 1->2 points straight North (no turn at waypoint 1); leaving waypoint k (k=2..N-1) applies sequence[k-2] then travels to k+1; exactly N-2 turns
Segment length randomised per segment between 60px and 140px, scalable up to 8x to satisfy spacing
Wildcards: max(1, round(count/9)) per walk, randomised positions among waypoints 2..N-1; a wildcard skips that waypoint's turn (heading unchanged)
Only the intended turn is attempted - no opposite-turn / straight / 180-degree fallback
Lookahead: before committing a position for waypoint i, verify a valid position remains for i+1 (and i+2); skip dead-end candidates
Bounded control flow: 200 placement attempts per size -> grow bounds 10% up to ~10 times -> re-randomise the whole turn sequence up to ~20 times -> return failure signal; termination is guaranteed

Headless tests with a seeded RandomSource verify: generation terminates; produced walks are valid across counts including 10 and 90; the failure signal is reachable; output is deterministic for a fixed seed
Tests pass (npm test)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: src/domain/walk-generator.ts: `walkGenerator.generate(count, random, config?)` is a `function*` yielding GenerationProgress {attempts, rerolls} (once per re-roll + every yieldEvery=50 attempts) and returning GenerationResult ({ok:true, walk} | {ok:false, reason, attempts, rerolls}). Works on plain Point[]/Segment[] buffers; wraps the finished placement in Walk.create ONCE (no draft Walk). Bounded loops (ADR-0002): maxPlacementAttempts=200 per size -> grow bounds 1.1x up to maxGrowths=10 -> re-roll whole turn sequence up to maxRerolls=20 -> failure. placeOnce is a bounded-backtracking DFS over per-segment length multipliers (base nextInt(60,140) * mult 1..maxScale=8), capped at maxPlacementSteps=6000 — GREEDY placement dead-ended ~90% for count=90; backtracking made it 30/30 and ~9ms avg. Lookahead (canExtend) verifies i+1 and i+2 are placeable with fixed probe lengths (NO randomness, keeps stream deterministic). Direction is fixed by the heading (only length scales) so only the intended turn is ever applied. Wildcards: wildcardCountFor(count)=min(max(1,round(count/9)), count-2) interior indices via partial Fisher-Yates. Placement is relative+centred-in-bounds (spacing is translation-invariant; within-bounds is a size check after centring); growth fixes size, re-roll fixes spacing (early-break when a size yields no spacing-valid placement). Refactored layout-rules.ts to EXPORT turnLabelPoint(position:Point) so the generator's incremental conflict check shares the exact NE-label geometry. tests/walk-generator.test.ts: 22 tests. REFINEMENT: hardened the input guard to reject non-integer/non-finite counts (`!Number.isInteger(count)`) with a clear RangeError instead of the opaque 'Invalid array length' thrown later by `new Array(2.5)` in placement. Added tests for count=2 (single straight-North segment, no interior/wildcards) and count=3 (sole interior is the wildcard -> two collinear North segments, still a valid Walk), a segment-length decomposition test (every length = base∈[60,140] × mult∈[1,8], validating the '60-140px scalable to 8x' AC directly), a progress-monotonicity test (attempts/rerolls non-decreasing, one yield per re-roll), and a non-integer-guard test. 221 total pass. REFINEMENT 2 (tests only, no impl change): added 3 tests (25 walk-generator, 224 total) closing AC gaps the prior passes left untested — (a) bounds-growth-rescue: with a 200x200 initialRegion too small to fit a count=10 walk, maxGrowths:3 exhausts to failure but the default maxGrowths:10 succeeds for the SAME seed (4242), isolating ADR-0002's middle 'grow bounds 10% up to ~10 times' clause which earlier tests only hit via maxGrowths:0 (the failure path); (b) wildcard-position randomisation: count=90 (10 wildcards) scatters them to different interior indices across seeds 1 vs 2 — prior tests pinned the wildcard COUNT and interior-ness but not the 'randomised positions' AC; (c) DEFAULT_CONFIG guard: pins the exported budget to ADR-0002 (200/10/20, 1.1, 60-140, 8x) so a stray edit is caught. REFINEMENT 3 (tests only, no impl change): added 1 test (26 walk-generator, 225 total) closing the LAST untested half of the progress-yield AC. The AC reads 'roughly every batch (~50 attempts, OR once per re-roll)'; prior tests only covered the per-re-roll half (the monotonicity test uses maxPlacementAttempts:2, never reaching a 50-attempt batch boundary). New test reuses the impossible-fit setup (100x100 region, maxGrowths:0, count=10) but with maxPlacementAttempts:120 so re-roll 0 runs all 120 attempts without fitting — the default yieldEvery=50 then emits batch yields at attempts 50 and 100 (both with rerolls===0), asserted via deepEqual. Same test also pins the failure signal's running totals (attempts===120, rerolls===1) since US-020 surfaces them — previously only `rerolls` and the reason string were asserted, never `attempts`. Deterministic and non-fragile: nothing fits 100x100 so the inner loop always runs to its cap, independent of placeOnce's per-attempt success.

userStories.title: GenerateWalk use case
userStories.description: As a user action handler, I need a use case that drives the generator iterator and cooperatively yields so the UI can paint during long generations.
acceptanceCriteria: GenerateWalk depends only on domain + ports; it drives the walk-generator iterator to completion, awaiting the injected Yield port's yieldToEventLoop() between batches
It ignores the generator's yielded progress value (the overlay spinner animates via CSS once the event loop is freed)
Returns the valid Walk on success, or surfaces the failure signal for the caller to render an error
Headless tests use an immediate/no-op Yield port and a seeded RandomSource; assert success returns a valid walk and the exhausted-re-roll path surfaces failure
Tests pass (npm test)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: src/application/generate-walk.ts: `GenerateWalk` class. Constructor injects ONLY the Yield port (the one stable dependency); `async execute(count, random, config?)` takes the per-generation RandomSource (kept seed-agnostic so US-022 can pass a fresh SeededRandom(seed) per click) + an optional GeneratorConfig passthrough (used by tests to force the bounded failure path). Drives `walkGenerator.generate(...)` one `.next()` at a time, `await`ing `eventLoopYield.yieldToEventLoop()` between every yield so the browser can paint the loading overlay before the next synchronous placement chunk; the yielded GenerationProgress is DISCARDED (overlay spinner is pure CSS). Returns the terminal GenerationResult verbatim — {ok:true,walk} on success, {ok:false,reason,attempts,rerolls} on exhausted re-rolls (US-020 renders the latter). Depends only on domain + ports (no DOM — enforced by tsconfig.core having no DOM lib). The generator's count guard fires on the first `.next()`, so a bad count surfaces as a REJECTED promise (RangeError) from execute. Field is named `eventLoopYield` not `yield` (reserved word in module/strict scope). tests/generate-walk.test.ts: 9 tests — success returns a valid Walk (count 10 & 90); yields >=1 time via a CountingYield; faithful-driver (use-case result === synchronously-driven generator result for the same seed, also proving progress is discarded); determinism (same seed twice); exhausted-re-roll failure surfaces {ok:false}/reason; >=1 yield per re-roll on the failure path; RangeError rejection for count<2 and non-integer count. 234 total pass. REFINEMENT (tests only, no impl change): added 2 tests (11 generate-walk, 236 total) closing two implemented-but-unasserted gaps. (a) EXACT yield count: the use case must await the Yield port exactly once per generator progress value (between every .next(), never after the done step) — prior tests only asserted >=1 (success) / >=3 (failure). New test parametrises [10/4242, 90/4242, 40/999] and asserts counting.calls === countGeneratorYields(...) computed from the SAME generator (self-consistent, seed-robust, not pinned to a constant); also tightened the existing failure-yield test to === countGeneratorYields(10,7,EXHAUSTING_CONFIG)=3. (b) FAILURE-path faithful driver: deepEqual(useCaseResult, directlyDrivenResult) for EXHAUSTING_CONFIG pins the WHOLE failure object surfaced verbatim — reason, rerolls, and crucially attempts (=9, which US-020 surfaces) — not merely ok===false; the success-path faithful-driver test already covered the happy path, this mirrors it for failure. REFINEMENT 2 (tests only, no impl change): added 1 test (12 generate-walk, 237 total) closing the AC clause every prior test left untested — that the loop GENUINELY cedes the event loop BETWEEN batches (the 'so the UI can paint'/'once the event loop is freed' purpose). All prior tests used a microtask Yield (Promise.resolve / async++) and asserted only the COUNT of awaits or the result; none proved a real macrotask boundary interleaves external work mid-generation. New test injects a setImmediate-based Yield and, on the 3-step EXHAUSTING_CONFIG failure path, queues an external setImmediate right after execute suspends; it must fire while generation is still in flight (finished===false). Deterministic via setImmediate FIFO + microtask-drain-between-macrotasks. Catches the 'batch then yield once at the end' and 'drain synchronously' regressions, which the count-based tests pass through.

userStories.title: ClearWalk use case
userStories.description: As a user action handler, I need a use case that clears the current walk.
acceptanceCriteria: ClearWalk resets the current-walk state to empty; depends only on domain + ports
Headless test asserts state is empty after clear
Tests pass (npm test)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: src/application/clear-walk.ts: `ClearWalk` use case. Constructor injects ONLY the Renderer driven port; `execute(): void` (SYNCHRONOUS — unlike GenerateWalk.execute which is async to yield around long generation) calls `renderer.clear()`. Per ADR-0003 this is a STATELESS generative toy — repository/persistence is 'deliberately omitted as unjustified ceremony', so there is no stored current-walk to mutate: the rendered view IS the current-walk state, and clearing it (removing every waypoint+line) through the Renderer port resets that state to empty. Depends only on application ports (no DOM/Canvas/domain mutation — mechanically enforced by tsconfig.core having no DOM lib), mirroring GenerateWalk's injected-collaborators/no-held-state shape. US-016 (dom-controls) calls execute() directly on the Clear button and before each Generate (no await — relies on the sync contract). tests/clear-walk.test.ts: 6 tests — state empty after clear (the AC, via a FakeRenderer whose `current: Walk|null` models 'what is on screen'); clear() reaches the port exactly once; clear never draws; idempotent on an already-empty state (both clears reach the port); generate-draw-clear cycles (counts 10/40/90) always end empty (proves the use case holds no cross-call state); execute is synchronous (returns undefined, not a Promise — pins the no-async contract US-016 depends on). 243 total pass.

userStories.title: Canvas renderer - grid, segments, waypoints
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see the walk drawn on the canvas so the generated route is visible.
acceptanceCriteria: Implements the Renderer port using the Canvas 2D API; DOM/Canvas usage confined to src/adapters/canvas-renderer.ts
Draws a subtle light-grey grid, 60px cells, covering the waypoints' bounding box with 100px padding on each side
Draws orthogonal path lines (no diagonals, no mid-segment corners), dark grey/black, 2px weight

Draws each waypoint as a radius-25px circle with number centred in bold Arial 20px: waypoint 1 and the last waypoint are black fill / white border / white number; all others white fill / black border / black number
Typecheck passes
Capture a screenshot via browser automation showing a generated walk with grid, connected orthogonal segments, and correctly styled numbered waypoints, as evidence for human sign-off
userStories.notes: src/adapters/canvas-renderer.ts: `CanvasRenderer implements Renderer` (the Canvas 2D output boundary; ALL DOM/Canvas confined here, compiled by tsconfig.adapters with the DOM lib). Constructor takes the HTMLCanvasElement, grabs the 2D context (throws if null). draw(walk, _options): fills white background in SCREEN space, then content box = walk.boundingBox grown by GRID_PADDING=100 each side, translate(-minX,-minY) inside save/restore so the walk is visible (US-015 layers A4 cap/downscale/centre on top of this plain translate). Grid: light-grey #e6e6e6, 1px, 60px lattice (Math.ceil(min/60)*60) clipped to the padded content box. Path: ONE connected polyline through waypoint centres, #222222, 2px — orthogonality guaranteed by the Segment ctor (corners only at waypoints, no diagonals/mid-segment bends). Waypoints: r=25 arc (radius imported from domain/layout-rules WAYPOINT_RADIUS — single source of truth), bold 20px Arial number centred; terminals (waypoint.isTerminal) black fill/white border/white number, interiors white fill/black border/black number; exactly first+last are black. clear(): resets transform + clearRect (US-012 ClearWalk). _options (showWildcards/showTurns) accepted but consumed from US-014 on. NO tests committed: tsconfig.test references core only so adapters can't be node:test-ed (that's why this story's ACs are typecheck + screenshot, not 'Tests pass'). VERIFIED: tsc -b clean; 243 prior tests still pass (no regressions); headless harness drove a REAL count=10 generated walk through the compiled renderer via a recording fake 2D context and asserted every primitive (10+10 grid lines lattice-aligned & inside the box; 1 moveTo + 9 lineTo orthogonal polyline; 10 r=25 circles; per-waypoint fill/border/number colours; exactly 2 black terminals; clear() wipes full canvas). VISUAL evidence: recorded ops replayed to SVG (faithful — transform stack tracked, not a reimplementation) and rasterized via qlmanage for count=10 (seed 4242) and count=20 (seed 99) — both show grid, connected orthogonal segments, correctly styled numbered waypoints. CAVEAT: a LIVE browser-automation screenshot could NOT be captured (no browser/Playwright MCP in this environment); the qlmanage rasterization of the real recorded draw-calls stands in, and a human browser sign-off remains (easiest once US-016/US-021 wire main.ts, or via the scratchpad harness). REFINEMENT: the headless harness is committed as scripts/verify-renderer.mjs (npm run verify:renderer) and is the renderer regression gate. Hardened it to verify AC2/AC3 from the RECORDED ops rather than re-deriving from the walk: (a) grid '60px cells' now asserts consecutive lattice lines are exactly 60px apart (was only %60===0 per line); (b) the orthogonal path is now checked against the actual path ops — exactly one beginPath subpath with a single moveTo at wp1 + n-1 lineTos through wp2..wpN in order, each step axis-aligned — proving connectedness, through-the-centres, and no-diagonal/no-mid-segment-corner from the renderer output, where the prior check only re-confirmed the walk data was orthogonal. Re-verified: tsc -b clean; npm test 243 pass; npm run verify:renderer passes for counts 2/10/20/90.

userStories.title: Canvas renderer - turn labels and wildcard rings
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see each waypoint's outbound turn and which waypoints are wildcards.
acceptanceCriteria: Outbound turn label (L, R, or W for wildcard) drawn at the fixed NE (45 degrees) position, 46px from the waypoint centre; first and last waypoints show no label
Wildcard waypoints show an orange ring, 3px stroke, at radius 30px outside the centre
Rendering honours the Show Turns and Show/Hide Wildcards display options independently (W label governed by Show Turns, ring by Show Wildcards)
Typecheck passes
Capture screenshots via browser automation showing labels clear of segments at NE, wildcard rings present, and the two toggles acting independently, as evidence for human sign-off
userStories.notes: src/adapters/canvas-renderer.ts: extended draw() with two new passes, drawn AFTER the US-013 base picture (grid/path/circles), inside the same translate, in order rings-then-labels (so a label is never occluded by a ring). drawWildcardRings(walk): for each wp.wildcard, an arc at WILDCARD_RING_RADIUS=30 (3px, orange #ff8c00) — gated by options.showWildcards. drawTurnLabels(walk): for each interior wp, a bold 16px Arial centred label at turnLabelPoint(wp.position) (IMPORTED from domain/layout-rules — the EXACT NE 45deg/46px geometry the invariant reserves clearance for, no divergence), colour #222222 — gated by options.showTurns. Label text via module fn turnLabelText(wp): null for terminals (first/last show no label), 'W' for wildcards (turn skipped/walker-straight; wildcard.outboundTurn is null by entity invariant), else 'L'/'R' from Turn.Left/Right. The two toggles are INDEPENDENT: showTurns governs ALL labels incl the wildcard 'W'; showWildcards governs only the orange ring. draw() now consumes options (was _options). VERIFIED: tsc -b clean; npm test 243 pass (no regressions — adapters carry no node:tests by design); npm run verify:renderer extended with verifyTurnsAndWildcards(10/20/90) asserting from the RECORDED ops across all 4 toggle combos — one L/R/W label per interior wp at its NE turnLabelPoint (terminals none), one orange r=30 3px ring per wildcard at its centre, labels present iff showTurns, rings present iff showWildcards, and the n circles + n numbers unaffected by either toggle; the US-013 number-count assertion was tightened to exclude L/R/W fillTexts. VISUAL evidence: drove the real compiled renderer into an SVG-emitting fake ctx (faithful output, not a reimpl) for count=20 seed=99 (2 wildcards: wp7, wp10) across all 4 toggle combos and rasterized via qlmanage — confirmed L/R/W labels at NE clear of segments, orange rings on the wildcards, terminals (1,20) unlabelled, and the toggles acting independently (W shows under showTurns even with wildcards off; rings show under showWildcards even with turns off). CAVEAT: a LIVE browser-automation screenshot could NOT be captured (no browser/Playwright MCP in this env, same as US-013); the qlmanage rasterization of the renderer's real draw-calls stands in, human browser sign-off remains (easiest once US-016/US-021 wire main.ts). REFINEMENT (verify harness only, no impl change): closed the count=2 ALL-TERMINAL boundary gap. verifyTurnsAndWildcards requires >=1 label AND >=1 ring so it structurally never runs for count=2 (every waypoint terminal -> 0 interior labels, 0 wildcards), leaving the degenerate 'first and last show no label' case unasserted (verifyDraw(2,..) only checked the waypoint-NUMBER count). Added to verifyDraw (renders with both toggles on) a cross-check: turn-label ops === interior-labelled count and ring ops === wildcard count -> for count=2 BOTH are 0, directly proving the all-terminal walk draws no L/R/W labels and no rings; for 10/20/90 it independently cross-checks verifyDraw's render against verifyTurnsAndWildcards's separate renders. Re-verified: tsc -b clean; npm test 243 pass; npm run verify:renderer passes 2/10/20/90.

userStories.title: A4 cap, uniform downscale, auto-centre, viewport fit
userStories.description: As a user, I want the map to always fit on one A4 page and on my screen, with the full walk centred (docs/adr/0005).
acceptanceCriteria: The rendered canvas is capped at A4 (794x1123px at 96 PPI)
The waypoints' bounding box (+100px padding) is uniformly scaled down to fit A4 when it exceeds those dimensions; walks already within A4 are not scaled up
The walk auto-centres after generation so the full route is visible
If the canvas is wider than the viewport, it CSS-scales down (preserving aspect ratio) so there is no horizontal scrolling
Scaling/fit are adapter-only transforms; the domain's generation-space coordinates are untouched
Typecheck passes
Capture screenshots via browser automation showing a 90-waypoint walk fitting within A4 and the viewport, a small walk not enlarged, and both centred, as evidence for human sign-off
userStories.notes: src/adapters/canvas-renderer.ts draw(): after the white background (screen space), the padded content box (walk.boundingBox grown by GRID_PADDING=100 each side) is fitted to A4 via a pure canvas transform — translate(offsetX,offsetY) -> scale(s,s) -> translate(-minX,-minY) inside save/restore. A4_WIDTH=794, A4_HEIGHT=1123 (@96 PPI) constants added. s = Math.min(1, capW/contentW, capH/contentH) where cap = min(A4, canvas dim); the min(1,..) CLAMP means walks within A4 keep natural size (never enlarged), only larger walks downscale (AC2). offset = (canvasDim - contentDim*s)/2 auto-centres so the padded box's screen centre is exactly the canvas centre (AC3, equal margins — verified numerically: count=2/10/90 all centre at (397,561.5)). All drawing stays in generation-space px; the domain coords are untouched (AC5) — the transform maps them. Stroke widths/ring radius/label offset are gen-space px and scale along with everything (dense sub-nominal spacing for big walks is intended per ADR-0005). AC4 viewport fit is CSS in index.html (canvas { max-width:100%; height:auto }): backing store stays A4, only the displayed element shrinks on a narrow viewport preserving aspect ratio (no horizontal scroll) — a stylesheet concern, not a Canvas-2D transform. VERIFIED: tsc -b clean; npm test 243 pass (no regressions); npm run verify:renderer extended with verifyA4Fit(2/10/20/90) — the fake ctx now tracks the full {a,d,e,f} affine (scale+translate) so recorded SCREEN coords (x/y) reflect the fit, while lx/ly stay generation-space (US-013/US-014 assertions unaffected). verifyA4Fit asserts from FIRST PRINCIPLES (golden A4 constants hard-coded in the harness, NOT imported): scale derived from the output (screen spread / gen spread of two circles) matches min(1,A4/content); scale<1 for count=90 (0.5603), ===1 for the smaller fitting walks; every circle's screen position equals the centred-fit transform of its gen centre; padded box lands within [0,A4]; equal opposite margins (centring property); generation coords unchanged. VISUAL evidence (qlmanage stand-in, no browser MCP): drove the real compiled renderer into an SVG fake ctx with a red dashed A4 page outline — count=2/10 at natural size centred, count=90 downscaled to fill A4 width and centred. CAVEAT (carried from US-013/US-014): a LIVE browser-automation screenshot could not be captured (no browser/Playwright MCP in this env); the qlmanage rasterization of the real draw-calls + the first-principles harness stand in, human browser sign-off remains (easiest once US-016/US-021 wire main.ts and the page is served). REFINEMENT (verify harness only, no impl change): closed the only AC1 gap the prior passes left — verifyA4Fit renders onto an A4-SIZED canvas, where cap = min(A4, canvas) = A4 = canvas, so it could NOT distinguish 'capped at A4' from 'capped at the canvas'; a regression dropping the Math.min(A4, canvas) (capping at the canvas) passed every A4-sized assertion. Parametrised makeFakeContext/renderToOps with optional (canvasW, canvasH) (default A4) and added verifyA4CapOnLargerCanvas(90, 4242): renders count=90 onto a canvas STRICTLY larger than A4 and big enough that the walk fits it at natural size (max(2*A4, ceil(content)+1) = 1588x2246), so a canvas-only cap would emit scale 1 (no downscale) while the A4 cap still downscales to 0.5603 — the two hypotheses diverge sharply. Asserts the output-derived scale equals the A4 cap (NOT the canvas cap of 1), the content screen box stays <= A4 (794x1123) though the canvas is far larger, and the walk is still centred over the LARGER canvas (equal opposite margins; centring uses the canvas dim, scale uses the A4 cap). PROVEN TO BITE: temporarily capping at canvas.width/height made the new check fail ('scale 1 is the A4 cap 0.5603, NOT the canvas cap 1') while all four A4-sized verifyA4Fit checks still passed — reverted. Re-verified: npm run clean + tsc -b clean; npm test 243 pass; npm run verify:renderer all assertions pass (incl. the larger-canvas A4-cap check). REFINEMENT 2 (verify harness only, no impl change): closed the binding-AXIS coverage gap. scale = min(1, A4_W/contentW, A4_H/contentH) but a walk's bbox has ONE aspect, so a single seed makes only ONE term bind — the harness's sole downscale case (count=90/4242) is WIDTH-bound (A4_W/cW=0.5604 < A4_H/cH=0.5874), so the A4_H/contentH term was NEVER the active constraint and a regression to scale=min(1,A4_W/contentW) (drop the height term) passed everything yet overflows A4 vertically on a tall walk. Added a HEIGHT-bound downscale case (count=30 seed=17: cW=652 fits A4 width at natural size, cH=1278 overflows → only the height term forces scale=0.8787; found by scanning seeds for contentH/contentW > A4_H/A4_W≈1.414). verifyA4Fit now derives the binding axis, asserts it fills its A4 edge EXACTLY (contentDim*scale === A4 dim: width-bound→794, height-bound→1123), and returns {count,seed,scale,bind}; a call-site coverage gate asserts the downscale set spans BOTH 'WIDTH' and 'HEIGHT' so the height case can't be silently dropped. PROVEN TO BITE: scale=min(1,capW/contentW) made count=30/17 fail ('scale 1 matches the A4-fit formula 0.8787') while every WIDTH-bound check still passed — reverted + npm run clean. Re-verified: tsc -b clean; npm test 243 pass; npm run verify:renderer all assertions pass.

userStories.title: DOM controls driving adapter
userStories.description: As a user, I want the control surface (Generate, Clear, Waypoints, toggles, Print) so I can drive the app.
acceptanceCriteria: DOM usage confined to src/adapters/dom-controls.ts; it wires the GenerateWalk and ClearWalk use cases

Generate Walk: clears the canvas and draws a freshly randomised walk; during generation the button is disabled and a loading overlay (spinner + 'Generating...') is shown over the canvas; both are restored in a finally when generation completes
Clear: removes all waypoints and lines from the canvas
Waypoints: number input, range 10-90, default 90; sets the count for the next generation
Show/Hide Wildcards: toggles the orange rings only; rings visible by default
Show Turns: toggles the L/R/W labels (first/last show none); visible by default
Print: opens the browser print dialog
Typecheck passes
Capture screenshots via browser automation showing every control behaving as specified, including the overlay during generation and the toggles updating the render, as evidence for human sign-off
userStories.notes: src/adapters/dom-controls.ts: `DomControls` — the INPUT boundary of the hexagon (all interactive DOM confined here, compiled by tsconfig.adapters with the DOM lib). Constructor takes EXPLICIT element refs (DomControlsElements) so it's unit-testable with fakes; static `DomControls.fromDocument(deps, doc=document)` is the ONLY document-touching path (resolves each element by the exported CONTROL_IDS map, THROWS 'required element #id not found' on any miss). Deps (DomControlsDeps) inject GenerateWalk + ClearWalk use cases, the Renderer port, and a `createRandom: ()=>RandomSource` FACTORY (fresh seeded source per generation — keeps the adapter seed-agnostic; US-021 supplies entropy default, US-022 layers ?seed=). generate(): setBusy(true) (disable Generate button + show #loading-overlay) SYNCHRONOUSLY before `await generateWalk.execute(count, createRandom())`, clearWalk.execute() first, draw the walk on ok:true, leave canvas cleared on ok:false (US-020 adds the error overlay), setBusy(false) ALWAYS in a finally (success/failure/throw); generate rethrows, the click listener owns the rejection via .catch(console.error) so it can't become an unhandled rejection. clear()=clearWalk.execute()+forget walk. print()=window.print(). Toggles (change listeners) redraw the SAME held currentWalk (Walk|null — a pure UI/view concern, NOT domain state per ADR-0003) with live displayOptions {showWildcards: wildcardsToggle.checked, showTurns: turnsToggle.checked}, never regenerating; no-op with no current walk (before first Generate / after Clear). readCount() parses the input + clamps to [10,90], blank/NaN→90. index.html: added .controls markup (Generate/Clear buttons, #waypoint-count number input min=10 max=90 value=90, #toggle-wildcards + #toggle-turns checkboxes both `checked`, Print button), .canvas-wrap (position:relative) holding the canvas + #loading-overlay (spinner + 'Generating...'), and the spinner @keyframes CSS (pure-CSS animation — animates once GenerateWalk frees the event loop). main.ts NOT wired (that's US-021's composition-root story); the controls become interactive once US-021 wires main.ts + auto-generates on load. VERIFIED: tsc -b clean (typecheck); npm test -> 243 pass (no regressions — adapters carry no node:tests by design); npm run verify:renderer all pass. New headless gate `npm run verify:controls` (scripts/verify-controls.mjs): drives a REAL GenerateWalk/ClearWalk through DomControls with fake elements + a recording fake Renderer — 11 checks: clear-then-draw on Generate + overlay/disabled DURING (asserted synchronously pre-await) restored after; click wiring runs a real count=90 generation to a draw; Clear clears (no draw); toggles redraw the SAME walk object with INDEPENDENT options; toggles no-op with no current walk; count input drives + clamps [10,90] across 7 values (5->10, 999->90, ''->90, '42abc'->42); Print calls window.print once; finally restores on the ok:false failure signal (no draw) AND on a thrown execute (generate rethrows); overlay persists across an async generation (event loop free, external setImmediate runs mid-flight) then restores; fromDocument resolves by id + throws on a missing element. CAVEAT (carried from US-013/14/15): no browser/Playwright MCP in this env, so the live screenshot for human sign-off is still pending — the headless harness stands in for the functional ACs; the screenshot is a review gate, easiest once US-021 wires main.ts and the page is served. REFINEMENT (verify harness + docs only, no impl change): closed the ONE un-faked seam — the index.html<->adapter contract. Every prior check used fake elements + a fake document (fromDocument's test builds its fake doc straight from CONTROL_IDS), so a drift between CONTROL_IDS and the real ids in index.html, or a change to AC-mandated markup the fakes bypass, would only surface in the live browser (no MCP here) while the gate stayed green. Added a 12th verify:controls check that READS the real index.html and asserts: every CONTROL_IDS value exists as an id= (fromDocument won't throw at startup); the waypoint input is type=number min=10 max=90 value=90 (AC4 range/default); both toggles are checkboxes checked by default (AC5/AC6 visible-by-default); the overlay has a .spinner + 'Generating...' text + @keyframes spin (AC2). Golden values (10/90/90, 'Generating...') HARD-CODED in the harness, not imported, so a markup drift fails the gate. PROVEN TO BITE then reverted: rename #generate-button / value 90->50 / drop toggle `checked` / remove @keyframes spin each fired its AssertionError; clean run = 12/12. tsc -b clean; npm test 243 pass; verify:renderer + verify:controls green. REFINEMENT 2 (impl + harness): closed a finally-coverage gap in generate(). Previously setBusy(true) ran, then clearWalk.execute()+currentWalk=null ran OUTSIDE the try — a throw in the clear step would skip the setBusy(false) finally and STRAND the overlay+disabled button. Moved the whole operation (now via this.clear(), DRY-ing the duplicated clear logic) INSIDE the try so ANY failure restores the controls; nothing between setBusy(true) and the try can strand the busy state. Added two verify:controls checks (12->14): (a) a thrown CLEAR step still restores overlay/button in finally and aborts before any draw (generate rethrows) — PROVEN TO BITE: reverting to clear-outside-the-try left disabled===true after the throw (actual:true/expected:false); (b) each Generate pulls a FRESH source from createRandom exactly ONCE (randomsCreated increments 1 then 2 across two generates) — locks the seed-agnostic/no-shared-source contract US-022's single-seed reproducibility depends on, which no prior test asserted. tsc -b clean; npm test 243 pass; verify:renderer + verify:controls (14/14) green.

userStories.title: Waypoint click tooltip and hover highlighting
userStories.description: As a user, I want to click a waypoint for its details and get hover feedback so I can inspect the walk.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking a waypoint shows a DOM-overlay tooltip (not painted on canvas) with: waypoint number, turn direction (L / R / Wildcard), and cumulative distance from the start in generation-space px (stable across resizes)
Tooltip dismisses on Clear, on Generate, and on a click on empty canvas; it survives redraws
Hit-testing inverts both transforms (viewport -> A4 -> generation) to map the click to a waypoint
Hover over a waypoint: cursor becomes a pointer, the waypoint gains a drop shadow, and its connecting segments thicken to 4px; moving off the canvas removes all hover highlighting
Typecheck passes

Capture screenshots via browser automation showing a correct tooltip on click, the tooltip surviving a window resize and dismissing on the specified actions, and hover effects engaging and clearing on exit, as evidence for human sign-off
userStories.notes: Hit-testing + hover + tooltip split across the two adapter files. NEW src/adapters/interactive-renderer.ts: `InteractiveRenderer extends Renderer` adds hitTest(clientX,clientY):Waypoint|null and highlight(waypoint|null):void — deliberately NOT on the application Renderer port (picking/hover are view concerns no use case needs; adding them there would force tests/clear-walk.test.ts's Renderer fake to grow). Signatures use only numbers + a domain Waypoint, so DomControls stays port-typed + fake-testable. src/adapters/canvas-renderer.ts: `CanvasRenderer implements InteractiveRenderer`. draw() now stores lastWalk/lastOptions + resets the highlight + calls a shared private render(); render() computes & STORES the A4 fit {scale,offsetX,offsetY,minX,minY} ONCE. hitTest inverts BOTH transforms (AC3): (1) CSS element scale via getBoundingClientRect — backing=(client-rect.origin)/rect.size*canvas.size (the canvas is displayed smaller than its 794x1123 backing store on a narrow viewport per US-015 CSS); (2) the A4 fit — gen=(backing-offset)/scale+min; then nearest waypoint within WAYPOINT_RADIUS (unique since the layout invariant keeps circles >=50px apart). Returns null before any draw / over empty canvas / zero-sized element. highlight(wp) re-renders via render() with the hover: drawHighlightedSegments draws the hovered wp's incident segment(s) at 4px AFTER the 2px base path but BEFORE the circles (circles cover the endpoints); drawWaypoints gives the hovered circle a drop shadow SCOPED in its own ctx.save()/restore() so it never bleeds onto later circles or the number (number drawn after the restore, crisp). clear() nulls lastWalk/fit/highlight. src/adapters/dom-controls.ts: owns the canvas (#walk-canvas) + tooltip (#waypoint-tooltip) — added to CONTROL_IDS/DomControlsElements/fromDocument; DomControlsDeps.renderer is now InteractiveRenderer. Wires canvas click (hitTest -> showTooltip for the waypoint, or hideTooltip over empty canvas), mousemove (hitTest -> cursor pointer/default + renderer.highlight; only on a CHANGE of hovered wp, so a 90-wp walk isn't re-rendered per pixel) and mouseleave (clearHover). Tooltip text = `Waypoint N` / `Turn: L|R|Wildcard|none (start/end)` / `{d} px from start` where d=round(walk.cumulativeDistanceTo(seq-1)) — GENERATION-space px, stable across redraws/resizes (AC1). Dismissed on Clear & Generate (both route through clear(), which now also hideTooltip()+clearHover()); SURVIVES a toggle redraw (separate DOM element rerender() never touches; rerender re-applies the hover highlight after the draw since a fresh draw resets the renderer highlight) (AC2). index.html: #waypoint-tooltip overlay div in .canvas-wrap (position:absolute; display:none; pointer-events:none so it can't swallow the next canvas click; white-space:pre-line renders the \n-joined text as lines); #loading-overlay got z-index:2 to stay above it. VERIFIED: tsc -b clean; npm test 243 pass (no regressions — application Renderer port unchanged, so core tests untouched). verify:renderer EXTENDED: verifyHitTest drives the REAL renderer — converts each waypoint's recorded SCREEN centre to a client coord via a given rect (incl. a HALF-SIZE OFFSET rect to exercise the CSS-scale inversion, and an A4-downscaled count=90 to exercise the fit inversion) and asserts hitTest returns it; empty->null; before-draw->null. verifyHighlight asserts the 4px incident-segment path through the correct neighbours, the drop shadow on the highlighted fill but NOT on the number nor a LATER circle (catches shadow bleed), highlight(null) clears, before-draw no-op. Required upgrading makeFakeContext's save/restore to model the FULL state stack (matrix + styles incl. new shadow* keys) — a matrix-only restore leaked the shadow & silently passed the scoping regression. verify:controls EXTENDED to 20 checks: click shows the tooltip with number/turn/distance positioned at the click + forwards event coords to hitTest; Start tooltip (distance 0); wildcard tooltip (Turn: Wildcard); dismiss on empty-click/Clear/Generate + survives a toggle redraw; hover sets pointer cursor + highlight, no redundant re-highlight within the same wp, restores over empty canvas; mouseleave clears hover; index.html tooltip overlay CSS contract. Both new renderer gates PROVEN TO BITE then reverted (drop the rect-scale division -> half-rect hitTest fails; unscope the shadow -> 'number carries no shadow' fails). CAVEAT (carried from US-013/14/15/16): no browser/Playwright MCP in this env, so the LIVE screenshot for human sign-off remains pending; also the click/hover only become interactive once US-021 constructs DomControls (wires main.ts). The headless harnesses stand in for the functional ACs. REFINEMENT (verify:controls only, no impl change): closed two implemented-but-unasserted AC clauses (20 -> 22 checks). (a) END-waypoint tooltip: the prior gate clicked an interior wp, the Start (isFirst -> 'none (start)') and a wildcard, but NEVER the End wp, so turnDescription's isLast branch ('none (end)') had no guard; new test clicks the last wp and asserts 'Turn: none (end)' AND that its distance is the walk's TOTAL (round(cumulativeDistanceTo(N-1)) === round(totalDistance)). (b) AC1 'stable across resizes': the distance is generation-space px sourced from the domain Walk, never screen pixels; new test clicks an interior wp, mutates the fake canvas getBoundingClientRect (half-size + shifted origin, exactly US-015's CSS scale on a narrow viewport), re-clicks the SAME wp at the SAME client coords, and asserts the 'N px from start' line is byte-identical (only the on-screen tooltip POSITION moved, asserted via notDeepEqual so the test isn't vacuous). BOTH PROVEN TO BITE then reverted: isLast->'none' fails the End test; sourcing distance from rect.width fails 'distance line is unchanged after the resize'. Reverted via precise Edits (NOT git checkout — the story files are uncommitted) + npm run clean before re-running. tsc -b clean; npm test 243 pass; verify:renderer + verify:controls (22/22) green. REFINEMENT 2 (impl + verify:renderer): made hitTest BORDER-AWARE — a genuine AC3 fix. getBoundingClientRect() returns the element's BORDER box, but the canvas backing store maps to its CONTENT box, and index.html gives #walk-canvas a 1px border; the old inversion `(client-rect.left)/rect.width*canvas.width` treated the border box AS the content box, skewing the viewport→backing mapping off-centre (negligible at full size with a 1px border, but growing as the element is CSS-downscaled since the border doesn't scale with the content). Fixed to subtract the uniform border from BOTH origin and size: `borderX=canvas.clientLeft; contentW=rect.width-2*borderX; backing=(client-rect.left-borderX)/contentW*canvas.width` (guard contentW/H<=0); clientLeft/clientTop are 0 on an unbordered canvas so it reduces to the prior mapping. verifyHitTest gained an optional `border` param (the fake canvas models a border box with matching clientLeft/clientTop/clientWidth/clientHeight; client coords derived through the CONTENT box) + a `border 24` half-size count=90 case. PROVEN TO BITE then reverted: the old border-box mapping fails the border-24 case at wp1 (outer waypoint falls outside WAYPOINT_RADIUS of its true centre) while every border-0 case still passes. NB the canvas CENTRE maps exactly even with the bug (symmetric border cancels), so the proof needs OFF-CENTRE targets on a downscaled element. verify:controls unaffected (it uses a fake renderer); dom-controls tooltip positioning intentionally not border-corrected (only lands near the click, never inverts to a gen coord). tsc -b clean; npm test 243 pass; verify:renderer + verify:controls (22/22) green.

userStories.title: Legend (DOM/HTML)
userStories.description: As a user, I want a legend explaining the symbols.
acceptanceCriteria: A legend rendered as DOM/HTML (not canvas-painted) below the canvas, with three entries: Start / End (black filled circle), Waypoint (white filled circle, black border), Wildcard (orange ring - walker goes straight)
Typecheck passes
Capture a screenshot via browser automation showing the legend below the canvas with the three correct entries, as evidence for human sign-off

userStories.title: Print stylesheet (single A4 page)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to print just the map and legend cleanly.
acceptanceCriteria: A print stylesheet prints the canvas and legend on a single A4 page with all other UI chrome hidden
Typecheck passes
Capture a print-preview screenshot via browser automation showing only the map + legend on one A4 page, as evidence for human sign-off

userStories.title: Graceful generation-failure error
userStories.description: As a user, if generation can't find a valid walk, I want a clear message rather than a hang.
acceptanceCriteria: When GenerateWalk surfaces the exhausted-re-roll failure signal, an error is shown over the canvas: 'Couldn't generate a walk - try again or reduce the waypoint count', and the controls are restored
The UI never hangs: this path is reached via the bounded generator, not an infinite loop
Typecheck passes
Capture a screenshot via browser automation (forcing or simulating the failure path) showing the error overlay and re-enabled controls, as evidence for human sign-off

userStories.title: Composition root (main.ts) and auto-generate on load
userStories.description: As a user, I want the app fully wired and showing a walk the moment the page loads.
acceptanceCriteria: src/main.ts is the composition root: it instantiates the domain/application layers and the adapters (canvas renderer, DOM controls), injects the production RandomSource (entropy-seeded by default; the ?seed= URL override is layered on in US-022) and a Yield port whose yieldToEventLoop() is a macrotask in production
A walk is automatically generated on first page load, so the canvas is never blank
npm run build typecheck passes; npm test still passes (no regressions)
Typecheck passes
Capture a screenshot via browser automation showing the page (served via npm run serve) with an auto-generated walk on load and all controls working end-to-end, as evidence for human sign-off

userStories.title: Shareable reproducible walks via ?seed= URL
userStories.description: As a user, I want a URL that reproduces the exact walk I'm looking at so I can share or revisit a specific generation.
acceptanceCriteria: On load, if the URL has a ?seed= parameter (and optional waypoint count), the production RandomSource is seeded with it so the identical walk is regenerated; with no seed param, behaviour is unchanged (entropy-seeded fresh walk)
After each Generate Walk, the URL is updated (without reloading) to reflect the seed and waypoint count that produced the current walk
The seed used for a generation is recoverable - opening the reflected URL reproduces the same waypoints, turns, segment lengths, and wildcards
URL read/write lives in the adapter/composition layer only; the domain stays seed-agnostic
A headless test confirms the same seed yields an identical Walk (determinism)
Tests pass (npm test)
Typecheck passes
Capture screenshots via browser automation showing two loads of the same ?seed= URL produce visually identical walks and the URL updating after a fresh Generate, as evidence for human sign-off
